title: Buyer beware! Deceptive billing practices!
body: I enrolled in Sleep Reset following a referral from my primary physician. During onboarding, I selected my insurance provider, and the app indicated that my insurance was accepted. This created a clear expectation that services would be eligible for reimbursement, and I relied on that representation when deciding to proceed. At no point was I asked to verify my insurance details or informed that additional steps would be required. I was directed straight into payment and began using the service. I later confirmed directly with my insurance that Sleep Reset was not in-network and that reimbursement would require a Superbill. You can imagine my shock when I requested this from customer support, only to learn that I could not receive one because I had not completed a live consultation. This was the first time I had ever been made aware of that requirement. The app never prompted me to schedule a consultation, and there was no clear path to do so. When I raised this with support, they acknowledged that the app should not have shown my insurance as accepted and stated that I should have been prompted to schedule a consultation. Neither of those things occurred. In total, I paid $1,500 to Sleep Reset under the false expectation that services would be reimbursable. Despite the financial impact, the only resolution offered was a $75 refund, which is comparable to burning someone's house down and offering an Amazon gift card to rebuild it. After sharing my experience online, I received a flood of messages from numerous former patients who described similar situations. There were various accounts of users being told their insurance was accepted during onboarding, only to later learn it was not. Others described being charged earlier than expected during free trials, subscriptions continuing beyond the advertised program length without clear notice, or discovering only during cancellation attempts that there were lower-cost plan options that had not been presented upfront. All of this paints a grim picture of a healthcare provider financially preying on sleep-deprived patients with deceptive marketing and billing practices. I strongly encourage anyone that's thinking about using Sleep Reset to carefully re-consider and choose another platform that's more transparent about their pricing.

title: Game changer…get this app
body: This app and its personalized programs are life changing. If you follow through it will completely transform your sleep. Here's my story…I started suffering from insomnia about 1.5 years ago. It started off just a day or so a month, then a day a week, then every day until it resulted in cases where I would get no sleep or only about 20-30 min a day. I tried everything from OTC meds, to various devices, to pot as well as the heaviest of Rx that did literally nothing for me. During a visit to ER, a nurse told me about CBT. I hadn't heard about it before and figured it was just another weird therapy that wouldn't do anything so I dismissed it. It wasn't until I had night after night of 30 min of sleep and being a zombie to my family that I did some research and came across two CBT-I apps. I elected to choose Sleep Reset because it had a personalized coach. I started the program, but honestly didn't notice any change and got really frustrated. After about a month in, I hadn't noticed a difference. I consulted with coach who was equally puzzled but I told her my issue with the sleep compression schedule and we made an adjustment. After about 6 weeks into the program I started to notice gradual change.

title: I Always Needed 30 Minutes to Fall Asleep, Until I Tried the Sleep Reset App
body: The first few days of my compressed sleep schedule were miserable and filled with brain fog, exhaustion, and irritability. Feeling hopeless, and frankly tortured, I messaged Jill for a pep talk. I begged her for an end date to the sleep compression phase and, much to my chagrin, she explained it all had to do with my consistency and commitment. In short, the closer I stuck to the plan, the less time I'd have to endure this phase. Jill noted that this could take several weeks. While I did not experience app crashes, I did struggle with glitchiness fairly often. For example, the app would freeze or I would tap a button to no avail. Cons: Could be cost-prohibitive for some. Program is intense, and takes work and dedication. App can be glitchy.

title: I hit bottom - this app is tops!
body: Easy start, no physician referral, but hmmm, a little pricey. Well… it works!!! I'm about 5 weeks in and am now consistently getting 6.5 hours continuous restorative sleep. It's not cheap and the first couple of weeks were tough but this program really works.

title: Good content helped me a lot
body: The reset sucked for the first few days but when it started to work it is amazing.

title: Sleep improved in 4 weeks
body: Although it is expensive I'm definitely going to continue because it has worked for me and I want to continue to improve and create long term sleep hygiene.
